channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Ex-member of former Gambia dictator's military unit is on trial in the US for torture
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Opening statements are expected Tuesday in the trial of a man accused of torturing political opponents of Gambia's former military dictator Yahya Jammeh nearly 20 years ago, the latest international trial tied to his regime.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How Philadelphia police draw on personal experiences to respond to mental health crises
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — On a frigid February morning, Vanity Cordero, a Philadelphia police officer, heard a call over the radio for a man threatening to jump from a bridge. The details sounded familiar.
item.dc:creator: CLAUDIA LAUER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: RFK Jr. plans to tell CDC to stop recommending fluoride in drinking water
item.description: The addition of low levels of fluoride to drinking water was long considered one of the greatest public health achievements of the last century.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Federal magistrate weighs arguments in Idaho news groups' execution access lawsuit
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — A federal magistrate judge is expected to hear arguments Tuesday morning in a lawsuit brought by three news organizations that say Idaho prison officials are unconstitutionally hiding parts of lethal injection executions from public view.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Utah Red Cross volunteers deployed to assist with flooding, tornado relief in several other states
item.description: Volunteers with the American Red Cross of Utah disaster team have been deployed to several states that have been affected by recent tornadoes and flooding in the Midwest and South.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Tennessee repeat felon charged in Rhodes College student's 2021 murder learns his fate
item.description: Memphis, Tennessee, felon Rainess Holmes has been sentenced in connection with Rhodes College student Andrew Rainer's October 2021 murder during a home invasion.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China threatens countermeasures after Trump threatens additional 50% tariff
item.description: China threatened Tuesday to take countermeasures against the United States "to safeguard its own rights and interests” in response to President Donald Trump’s threat of an additional 50% tariff on Chinese imports.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Duke basketball star could be deported under Trump visa policy
item.description: The State Department announced Saturday that visas of South Sudanese passport holders would be revoked.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Johnny Gaudreau's wife Meredith announces birth of couple's third child
item.description: Johnny and his brother were killed in New Jersey when they were riding bicycles and struck from behind by a suspected drunk driver.
item.dc:creator: Orri Benatar, NewsNation

item.title: Fan-favorite Krispy Kreme item returning for Easter
item.description: Krispy Kreme is celebrating Easter with an all-new doughnut collection, which includes a fan-favorite item. Starting Monday, fans can enjoy the all-new Hoppy Easter Collection, featuring three festive doughnuts and the strawberry doughnut.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Mega Millions tickets rise to $5, but the lottery promises more giant jackpots
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Mega Millions players will get slightly better odds and should start seeing more billion-dollar jackpots, but at a cost — literally — with tickets for the multistate lottery jumping in price to $5.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT McFETRIDGE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Buying Bitcoin? Here's what to know, according to the Utah Dept. of Commerce
item.description: If you're considering investing in cryptocurrency, the Utah Dept. of Commerce has some advice to help you avoid becoming victim of a scam.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration asks Supreme Court to block order to return Maryland man from El Salvador
item.description: The Trump administration on Monday asked the Supreme Court to block a court order requiring it to return a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported to a notorious prison in El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Distressed kitten rescued, adopted by woman who called for help
item.description: But after crews rescued the feline, named "Flush," from a storm drain, the woman fell in love with him and decided to adopt him.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, NewsNation

item.title: YPD: Man locks himself in store overnight, hauls items to door, calls 911 to get let out
item.description: Aaron Jones, 30, was booked into the Mahoning County jail on a charge of breaking and entering, a fifth-degree felony.
item.dc:creator: Joe Gorman, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Idaho mom who killed 2 of her kids goes on trial over husband's death
item.description: Lori Vallow Daybell, the Idaho mother with doomsday religious beliefs who was convicted of killing her two youngest children and conspiring to murder a romantic rival, is on trial again. This time, she's accused in Arizona of conspiring to murder her estranged husband.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump threatens additional 50 percent tariff on China
item.description: The White House had already imposed a 20 percent tariff on Chinese imports over fentanyl concerns, meaning China could face an effective tariff rate as high as 70 percent.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: Louisiana shrimpers commend new tariffs, express optimism
item.description: Louisiana shrimpers are hopeful the new tariffs on imported shrimp will bolster the domestic shrimp industry.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Hamilton, NewsNation

item.title: Harriet Tubman reference removed from National Parks' Underground Railroad web page
item.description: The National Parks Service (NPS) has removed a reference to abolitionist Harriet Tubman from its webpage dedicated to the Underground Railroad.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says he's not backing down, calls tariffs 'medicine' as markets reel
item.description: Speaking to reporters aboard Air Force One, Trump said he didn't want global markets to fall, but also that he wasn't concerned about the massive sell-off either.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: George M. Johnson's 'All Boys Aren't Blue' tops 2024 list of most 'challenged' US library books
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Removing books from library shelves is no longer just a story of objections from a local community or an individual parent, the American Library Association says.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pres. Nelson announces 15 new temples during April 2025 general conference
item.description: President Russell M. Nelson of The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints announced several new temples during the church's April 2025 general conference.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: At least 16 dead in flooding, tornadoes as storms slash parts of US
item.description: Many of the impacted areas already are heavily waterlogged by days of severe storms that spawned deadly tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: California town reports third fatality related to rare virus linked to death of Gene Hackman's wife
item.description: Investigators recently blamed the rare virus for the death of Betsy Arakawa, the wife of actor Gene Hackman.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: Florida charter captain arrested after viral altercation with young fisherman
item.description: Police in Punta Gorda spoke out about the incident Saturday, saying "this type of behavior will not be tolerated.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WGN 9

item.title: Trump administration argues judge can't order return of man mistakenly deported to El Salvador
item.description: A federal judge didn't have the authority to order the Trump administration to broker the return of a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported from the U.S. to a notorious El Salvador prison, government attorneys argued Saturday as they urged an appeals court to suspend the ruling.
item.dc:creator: ABC 4

item.title: Increasing sleepiness linked to dementia risk in women, study finds
item.description: The research by Neurology discovered women in their eighties who experience sleep disruption have an association with cognitive aging.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, ABC 4

item.title: Trump expected to host Netanyahu at White House next week
item.description: President Trump is expected to host Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu for a visit on Monday, a White House official told NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Federal officials are quietly terminating the legal residency of some international college students
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A crackdown on foreign students is alarming college leaders, who say the Trump administration is using new tactics and vague justifications to push some students out of the country. College officials worry the new approach will keep foreigners from wanting to study in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, ANNIE MA and MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Divided Supreme Court sides with Trump to block teacher grants
item.description: A divided Supreme Court sided with the Trump administration by allowing officials to block $65 million in teacher development grants frozen over concerns they were promoting diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) practices.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tornadoes will become more frequent in coming weeks. Here's what to know if your home is destroyed
item.description: An average of 1,200 tornadoes hit the United States yearly, and the country will probably get more killer supercells spawning tornadoes and hail as the world warms, according to a 2023 study.
item.dc:creator: LISA BAUMANN and MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Texas Pete hot sauces recalled in 10 states
item.description: The T.W. Garner Food Company issued a recall for certain bottles of its Texas Pete Habanero Buffalo Sauce and Texas Pete Sweet CHAbanero Sauce on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Reiner, WGN 9

item.title: What to know about the severe storms and flash flooding hitting parts of the US
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — At least seven people have been killed in a wide swath of violent storms and tornadoes that hit the South and Midwest, and officials are bracing for more severe weather and flooding in the coming days.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Comedian Russell Brand charged with rape and sexual assault in UK
item.description: British police on Friday charged Russell Brand with rape and sexual assault following an 18-month investigation sparked when four women alleged they had been assaulted by the controversial comedian.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump sued over China tariffs
item.description: It marks the first known legal challenge against Trump’s tariffs, which have fulfilled a campaign promise and rattled financial markets.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: Unemployment benefits for striking workers being considered in Oregon, Washington
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — Lawmakers in Oregon and Washington are considering whether striking workers should receive unemployment benefits, following recent walkouts by Boeing factory workers, hospital nurses and teachers in the Pacific Northwest that highlighted a new era of American labor activism.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE and CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Yankees OF Brett Gardner's son died of carbon monoxide poisoning, had 'layer' on organs, officials say
item.description: Randall Zúñiga, director of the Judicial Investigation Agency, said they tested the 14-year-old Miller Gardner for carboxyhemoglobin, a compound generated when carbon monoxide binds to hemoglobin in the blood.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WGN 9
